  • Hide All Code

MHC Class I Epitope Analysis Pipeline

Ibnu Ariyanto

20 April 2026

1 Overview

This report implements a comprehensive MHC Class I epitope analysis pipeline based on NetMHCpan output. It standardizes inputs, assigns binding classes, scores promiscuity across HLA alleles, filters candidates using simple physicochemical features, and exports publication-ready figures and tables.

2 1. Setup & Data Import

2.1 1.1 Packages

2.2 1.2 Input files

Update these paths to match your working directory.

2.3 1.3 Quick input checks

3 2. Binding Classification

NetMHCpan convention (EL %ile):

  • Strong binder (SB): EL %ile ≤ 0.5
  • Weak binder (WB): EL %ile ≤ 2.0
  • Non-binder (NB): otherwise
Binding class summary
binding_class n pct
Strong Binder (SB) 303 1.69
Weak Binder (WB) 628 3.51
Non-Binder (NB) 16970 94.80

4 3. Promiscuity Scoring

Scoring:

  • SB = 2 points
  • WB = 1 point
  • Promiscuous: binds ≥ 3 alleles (SB+WB)
## Top epitope candidates: 105

5 4. Visualizations

5.1 Fig 1 — Binding Distribution per Allele

5.2 Fig 2 — Promiscuity Score Histogram

5.3 Fig 3 — Binding Heatmap (Top 30 × All Alleles)

5.4 Fig 4 — Bubble Plot (Promiscuity vs Binding)

5.5 Fig 5 — Patient-Specific Allele Violin Plot

##        allele      binding_class  n
## 1 HLA-A*11:01 Strong Binder (SB)  9
## 2 HLA-A*11:01   Weak Binder (WB) 17
## 3 HLA-A*24:07 Strong Binder (SB) 15
## 4 HLA-A*24:07   Weak Binder (WB) 28
## 5 HLA-B*15:02 Strong Binder (SB) 23
## 6 HLA-B*15:02   Weak Binder (WB) 24
## 7 HLA-B*35:05 Strong Binder (SB) 18
## 8 HLA-B*35:05   Weak Binder (WB) 37
